author: yorickdowne
content:
Thanks for the guidance! This is the `config.toml` I have now for zkboost. I am running Lighthouse with an EL, but I don't want to force the user into `debug` namespace or a specific EL, so I'm downloading genesis.json instead

```
port = 3000
# Dummy to satisfy config requirements - if this is a Geth or Reth instead with debug_ namespace,
# zkboost will read debug_chainConfig
el_endpoint = "http://localhost:1"
# Chain config downloaded by entrypoint, means el_endpoint won't be used at startup
chain_config_path = "/var/lib/zkboost/network/config/genesis.json"

[[zkvm]]
kind = "verifier"
proof_type = "ethrex-openvm"
program_vk_url = "https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.13.0/stateless-validator-ethrex-openvm.vk"

[[zkvm]]
kind = "verifier"
proof_type = "ethrex-sp1"
program_vk_url = "https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.13.0/stateless-validator-ethrex-sp1.vk"

[[zkvm]]
kind = "verifier"
proof_type = "ethrex-zisk"
program_vk_url = "https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.13.0/stateless-validator-ethrex-zisk.vk"

[[zkvm]]
kind = "verifier"
proof_type = "reth-openvm"
program_vk_url = "https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.13.0/stateless-validator-reth-openvm.vk"

[[zkvm]]
kind = "verifier"
proof_type = "reth-sp1"
program_vk_url = "https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.13.0/stateless-validator-reth-sp1.vk"

[[zkvm]]
kind = "verifier"
proof_type = "reth-zisk"
program_vk_url = "https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.13.0/stateless-validator-reth-zisk.vk"
```

And then for Lighthouse run as usual, but add `--proof-engine-endpoint http://zkboost:3000`

author: yorickdowne
content: To have this work on mainnet, I am assuming I'd need specific bootnodes, or static peers, so that Lighthouse receives proofs via P2P. Are mainnet bootnodes for that purpose running? And what are they, if so?

author: han0110
content: Currently `zkboost` also depends on RPC endpoint `debug_executionWitness` to fetch execution witness of a payload, so far we have been testing with `reth` and only support the format of it, to support other EL we'd need to impl additional handler for each I think (e.g. `geth` has slight different format iirc). Do you have any specific EL you'd like to try?

author: yorickdowne
content: It does that for verification? Or is that only required when constructing proofs?

author: han0110
content:
Ah no, if only verification endpoint is used it won't need `debug_executionWitness`.

>  Or is that only required when constructing proofs?
Yes

author: yorickdowne
content: So, good enough for now. If there are mainnet nodes I can point to for bootnode or static node so I get proofs via P2P great; if not then that has time

author: yorickdowne
content: Moving the verifier into the CL eventually would make sense tbh. No need for a sidecar, and no need to somehow get the config from a `debug_` endpoint. The CL already knows chain config.

author: yorickdowne
content: No rush ofc, that's not real until Hegota

author: han0110
content:
I'm not aware of such proof generation node on mainnet now (perhaps <@402080271563751435> can confirm).

Yeah ideally CL would verify the proof locally without `zkboost`.

author: taulepton_
content: As far as I'm aware, no node operators are producing and gossiping proofs on mainnet. <@824439091004309535> mentioned that a fork of my lighthouse optional-proofs branch would be created for an ethproofs effort but I'm not sure what the ambition or status of that work is.

author: yorickdowne
content: Thanks! Early days yet. For now I have something to play with, and if/when proofs show up on P2P, I can try out the verifier. I appreciate the assistance!

author: taulepton_
content: most welcome - I think this is an interesting direction and I'm also curious if we can use the ethproofs to seed mainnet p2p - that would be <@824439091004309535> domain
